summary: Poland has moved from explanatory material towards an initial national framing of its quantum-safe posture. In August 2025 the Ministry of Digital Affairs published the foundational assumptions for a national quantum-technology development policy, structured across five pillars and three phases to 2035 and explicitly naming post-quantum security and post-quantum cryptography among its priorities. NASK, the national research institute and CSIRT operator, presents post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution as responses to the quantum threat, and the National Institute of Telecommunications (Instytut Lacznosci, IL-PIB) has produced introductory material, run seminars on post-quantum digital-signature standards, and committed to a security assessment of post-quantum cryptography solutions over 2026 to 2030. No binding national post-quantum migration mandate with dated algorithm-specific deadlines is yet attested; migration obligations rest on the EU framework and its coordinated roadmap.
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
migrationTimeline:
2026 | National Institute of Telecommunications (IL-PIB) begins a security assessment of post-quantum cryptography solutions (2026-2030 outlook)
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated (EU coordinated roadmap)
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete (EU coordinated roadmap); national quantum-technology policy phases run to 2035
govActors:
Ministerstwo Cyfryzacji (Ministry of Digital Affairs) | published the foundational assumptions for a national quantum-technology development policy naming post-quantum security and post-quantum cryptography as priorities
NASK | national research institute and CSIRT operator, presents post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution as responses to quantum threats
Instytut Łączności (National Institute of Telecommunications) | state research institute; introductory material, seminars on post-quantum digital-signature standards, and a planned 2026-2030 security assessment of post-quantum cryptography solutions
mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 (financial sector) | EU | binding-law
Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on a coordinated PQC roadmap | EU | soft-law
NIS Cooperation Group Coordinated Implementation Roadmap (2026/2030/2035) | EU | soft-law
obligation: Essential and important entities must use state-of-the-art cryptography under NIS2, transposed into national law, and financial entities face equivalent duties under DORA; neither yet names post-quantum algorithms specifically. Migration follows the EU coordinated roadmap, with high-risk use cases targeted for 2030 and full migration by 2035, which is encouraged rather than mandated.
classificationBasis:
NASK names PQC and QKD as responses to quantum threats: NASK QKD presentation (ingested)
Introductory explainer and seminar: Instytut Lacznosci items (ingested) - T4 research-institute material
RAD-on project profile and NASK Cyberpolicy US-strategy analysis: ingested but flagged as a research-database record and as analysis of another country, not used for posture - REVIEW

summary/standardFamilies/algorithms/hybridDeployment/timeline/targetCompletion omitted or minimal: no national migration policy stated in captured summaries
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
